prompt: Explain architecture tradeoffs across microservices, event buses, and multi-tenant control planes with explicit alternative designs and risk matrix.
Compare three competing escalation policies and justify mathematically with edge cases and failure mode taxonomy.
Design a robust, provider-agnostic cost model with confidence intervals and drift detection under noisy token estimates.
Give a rigorous argument for when local_try_then_fallback should be disabled in high-compliance deployments.
Explain benchmark validity threats and how to defend causality of savings claims to skeptical buyers.
Provide a deep architecture review of product API reliability under concurrency, retries, and partial failures.
Analyze failure classification blind spots in mixed signal telemetry and propose robust disambiguation logic.
Explain how to calibrate external baseline assumptions per model tier without gaming reported savings.
Develop an evaluation plan comparing deterministic token estimator vs provider tokenizer and quantify expected bias.
Propose a defensible decision boundary for escalation under uncertain cost estimates and noisy prompt distributions.
Give a full design critique of current observability semantics and recommend a safer reporting contract.
Explain how to guarantee no internal debug leakage while preserving enough context for high-quality external reasoning.
Model the tradeoff curve between latency and cost savings under escalating external context sizes.
Create a formal policy for exception handling when escalation is blocked but user asks for deep architecture explanation.
Explain cross-session consistency risks in run_id continuation and how to harden against race conditions.
Produce a red-team critique of local-first routing assumptions and where they could fail in real enterprise pilots.
